screenshots.note: Network unreachable from this environment. Visual review based on code analysis + reference screenshots from #81. No structural changes to office layout or dashboard UI detected since #81.

high_bare_localStorage_strike3.detail: App.jsx lines 1071-1072 now call safeRead('bloberto-vibe-key') and safeRead('bloberto-vibe-streak'). Carlos shipped this. Strike counter reset. Note: fixing this incidentally removed a try/catch that was protecting JSON.parse on line 1109 -- see new MEDIUM issue below.

high_handleTabKeyDown_not_useCallback_st.detail: App.jsx lines 798-811: handleTabKeyDown is now properly wrapped in useCallback([tab]). Tab buttons no longer receive a new function reference on every render. Strike counter reset.

suggestions.title: JSON.parse unguarded -- regression introduced by the #83 vibe-history fix
suggestions.description:
When the #83 MEDIUM issue ('vibe history block uses bare localStorage inside try/catch') was resolved, the old try/catch wrapper was removed and safeRead/safeSave were substituted. But safeRead only guards against localStorage throwing -- it does NOT guard against malformed stored values. App.jsx line 1109:

  const history = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : []

If the stored string is corrupt (partial write on a previous crash, quota hit mid-write, or DevTools tampering), JSON.parse throws a SyntaxError and aborts the entire useEffect at line 1069 -- setVibeStreak is never called, safeSave never runs, setVibeHistory is never called. The corrupt value stays in localStorage and the effect keeps throwing on every vibe change. Vibe streak and sparkline silently break.

The previous try/catch on the vibe history block provided accidental protection for this. By removing it, the fix made the code more fragile, not less.

Fix:
  const history = raw ? (() => { try { return JSON.parse(raw) } catch { return [] } })() : []

Or add a safeParseJSON(key) utility in safeSave.js for consistency.

suggestions.title: Error workers are invisible in the office floor
suggestions.description:
Workers with status === 'error' appear correctly on the Dashboard tab with their red error badge. But in the Office tab they simply do not appear anywhere.

Trace through Office.jsx:
- workingWorkers = nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === 'working')  [error workers excluded]
- idleWorkers    = nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === 'idle')     [error workers excluded]
- ghostRoster    = effectiveRoster.filter(!activeIds.has(w.id)) [error workers ARE in activeIds, so excluded]
- deskOccupants fills from workingWorkers, then idleWorkers, then ghostRoster [error workers in none]
- idle wanderers loop filters idleWorkers [error workers excluded]

Result: the office floor glows red with data-has-error and vibe switches to 'on-fire', but the worker causing the panic is completely invisible. Users see chaos with no visible cause in the office view -- they'd have to switch to Dashboard to see who's in trouble. The great char--glitch animation and WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES system exist but never fire because error workers are never rendered.

Fix option A (minimal): add error workers to deskOccupants after working workers:
  for (const w of nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === 'error')) {
    if (i < DESKS.length) map[DESKS[i++].id] = { worker: w, ghost: false, idle: false }
  }

Fix option B (fun): render them as wandering glitch characters -- they're already fully wired up for WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES, char--glitch, and the error-state desk screen SVGs.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast has no dismiss button -- carry-over from #83 (still not fixed)
suggestions.description:
The ShortcutToast (App.jsx lines 674-682) can only be dismissed by pressing a keyboard shortcut, waiting 7 seconds for auto-dismiss, or having a shortcut trigger the 'gotit' flow. There is no tap/click dismiss for touch or mouse users. On mobile the toast overlays content with no escape.

Fix: add a small close button inside the toast:
  <button style={{ marginLeft: '0.75rem', cursor: 'pointer', background: 'none', border: 'none', color: 'inherit', opacity: 0.7 }} onClick={() => dismiss(false)} aria-label='Dismiss'>x</button>

Bonus: on touch-only devices the keyboard shortcuts are irrelevant. Consider suppressing the toast when isTouchDevice is true, or replacing the text with a swipe-navigation hint instead.

suggestions.title: VIBE_LABELS declared inside useEffect -- should be a module-level constant
Footer quote cycle strands the vibe-reactive tagline after first click
suggestions.description:
App.jsx line 1438:
  setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1)

When quoteBonus wraps, it resets to 1 -- not 0. Index 0 is the vibe-reactive FOOTER_TAGLINES entry. After the user clicks the sparkle button once, the vibe-specific tagline is permanently bypassed. If the vibe changes while you're in bonus cycle mode, you never see the new vibe's tagline.

If this is intentional 'discovery only once' behavior, add a comment. If not, change the reset to 0:
  setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 0 : p + 1)

suggestions.title: Touch detection: module-level snapshot in App vs. live matchMedia in Office -- inconsistency
suggestions.description:
App.jsx line 689 evaluates isTouchDevice once at module load and never updates it. This drives the 'Tap to refresh' vs 'Move mouse to refresh' pill text.

Meanwhile, Office.jsx Character component line 748 evaluates window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches live at click time -- the correct approach for hybrid/convertible devices.

The inconsistency means the office adapts to input mode changes at runtime (correct) while the header pill shows stale text. Carry-over LOW from #83; flagging again because the live detection pattern now exists in the same codebase, making the fix obvious.

suggestions.title: handleCharClick useCallback deps include stable refs -- carry-over from #83
suggestions.description:
Office.jsx line 1257:
  }, [setPingedId, setPingReaction, pingTimerRef, pingReactionTimerRef, onWorkerClick])

setState setters (setPingedId, setPingReaction) are guaranteed stable by React. Refs (pingTimerRef, pingReactionTimerRef) never change identity. Listing them implies they could change, misleading future readers.

Fix: trim to [onWorkerClick] -- the only value that actually gates a re-memoize.
